postData.contentHtml:
Manila, Philippines – The Philippines has delivered a strong rebuke to recent claims by Chinese scholars suggesting that its strategically vital northernmost island province, Batanes, belongs to Beijing. Philippine Defense Minister Gilberto Teodoro Jr. did not mince words, outright rejecting these assertions as “baseless” and “ludicrous.”

The dispute centers around arguments made by scholars from institutions, including Nanjing University, who posited that Batanes is a natural geographical extension of Taiwan and, by inference, falls under China's territorial umbrella. This claim has been met with unified opposition from various Philippine government bodies.

The National Historical Commission of the Philippines (NHCP) has publicly refuted and condemned these claims, standing firm on the Philippines' historical and legal ownership of the island group. Further solidifying Manila's unwavering stance, the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) declared unequivocally that Philippine sovereignty over Batanes is “not up for debate.”

Batanes, situated north of Luzon and close to Taiwan, holds significant geopolitical importance. The Philippines' swift and decisive rejection underscores its commitment to defending its territorial integrity amidst ongoing regional maritime disputes.

On social media, the sentiment has largely been in favor of the Philippines' position, with many netizens expressing solidarity and support for the government’s firm stance, though some debate continues to circulate online.
postData.title: Philippines Fiercely Rejects 'Baseless' Chinese Claims Over Batanes Island
postData.summary: The Philippines has vehemently dismissed assertions by Chinese scholars claiming sovereignty over its northernmost island province of Batanes, calling them 'baseless' and 'ludicrous'.

sources.title: Philippines rejects Chinese scholars' claim over its island province near Taiwan
NHCP refutes scholars' claim that Batanes belongs to China
DFA: Philippine sovereignty over Batanes 'not up for debate'; NHCP rejects Chinese claims
